title: Bodrum Yacht Charter: Costs, Boats and Seasons for 2026
html:
Planning a yacht charter in Bodrum for 2026 means stepping into a turquoise paradise where the Aegean meets the Mediterranean. Daily rates for a crewed motor yacht start at around 2,500 TRY, with larger vessels commanding 10,000 TRY or more. Bodrum Castle and the Marina serve as the main departure points. BoatHire24 connects you with verified skippered charters across the region.

How much does it cost to rent a yacht in Bodrum?

Prices range from 1,500 TRY for a shared tour to over 15,000 TRY for a luxury crewed yacht. The table below breaks down typical options. Currency is Turkish Lira, and prices vary by season.

  • Shared gulet tour: 1,500, 2,500 TRY per person, including lunch and soft drinks.
  • Small motorboat half-day: 2,000, 4,000 TRY for up to 6 guests, no licence required if skippered.
  • Catamaran full-day: 6,000, 9,000 TRY for 8, 12 people, ideal for family groups.
  • Crewed motor yacht (e.g. Sunseeker 76): 10,000, 15,000 TRY for a full day with captain and crew.

For a wider selection and transparent pricing, compare verified listings for skippered charters in Bodrum on BoatHire24.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Bodrum?

For bareboat rental you need a valid ICC or equivalent, but skippered charters require no licence. Most holidaymakers opt for a captain-inclusive charter, which simplifies the experience. The boat operator will confirm exact requirements for your chosen vessel.

Book a sunset cruise from Bodrum Marina to the nearby Orak Island; the calm evening winds and golden light make it an unforgettable two-hour escape. Many skippers offer a light dinner on board.

Where to set off in Bodrum

Bodrum offers several outstanding departure points. The main Bodrum Marina sits at the heart of the town, with full facilities and dozens of charter boats. D-Marin Turgutreis, west of Bodrum, is a modern base with easy access to Greek islands. Torba Bay, just north, provides sheltered waters for beginners. For exploring the inner gulf, depart from Gümbet or Yalıkavak, each with its own marinas and lively waterfronts.

Which boat to rent

  1. Gulet (traditional wooden yacht), best for cruising the bays with a group of 8, 16 guests, from 3,000 TRY per day.
  2. Motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 55), ideal for fast island-hopping, 6, 10 guests, from 6,000 TRY per day.
  3. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 450), stable and spacious, perfect for families, 8, 12 guests, from 7,000 TRY per day.
  4. RIB or speedboat, for watersports and day-trips to Sedir Island, 4, 8 guests, from 2,500 TRY per half-day.
  5. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Mefasa 90), full crew and premium amenities, 10, 12 guests, starting at 10,000 TRY. Visit the Mefasa 90 charter page for details.

When is the best season for a yacht charter in Bodrum?

The high season runs from June to September, with July and August being the busiest and most expensive months. In 2026, expect consistent meltem winds that provide pleasant sailing conditions. May and October offer quieter anchorages and lower prices, though some boats still operate. Winter charters are rare; most boats are laid up from November to March.

At a glance

  • Price band: 1,500 TRY (shared tour) to 15,000+ TRY (luxury crewed yacht)
  • Peak months: June, September
  • Trip duration: Half-day (4 hours) to multi-day
  • Capacity: 4 (small speedboat) to 16 (gulet)
  • Currency: Turkish Lira (TRY)

Questions about boat rental in Bodrum

Can I rent a boat without a captain in Bodrum?

Yes, if you hold a recognised boating licence (e.g. ICC) and the rental company permits it, but most holidaymakers prefer hiring a skipper for peace of mind.

What is the best day trip from Bodrum by boat?

The most popular route takes you from Bodrum Marina to the Greek island of Kos, Orak Island for swimming, and the thermal springs of Kara Ada, typically a full-day excursion.

How far is Kos from Bodrum by boat?

The distance is only about 12 nautical miles, making it a straightforward one-hour crossing on a motor yacht, though customs clearance adds time.

Are children allowed on boat charters in Bodrum?

Yes, most skippered charters welcome children, and many operators offer safety vests and child-friendly itineraries; check with the provider when booking.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Bodrum or list your own. For a unique holiday business on the water, consider listing your vessel: become a host with BoatHire24 and reach thousands of travellers.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Bodrum and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
